items.title: Status pill sits above the title (no side-squeeze) on a long-title ticket
items.precondition: Logged in as a client (e.g. One Endodontics). Open Support / Help desk. Select the WEB-0011 ticket (long title about Dr. James Lee).
steps: 2. Click the WEB-0011 ticket with the long title.
3. Look at the ticket detail header on the right panel.
items.expected: The status pill (e.g. 'Waiting on you') appears on its own line ABOVE the ticket ID and title, left-aligned. It is not floated into a narrow right-hand column.
items.evidence: Verified on dev.getalloro.com via Pilot Mode (Artful Orthodontics, WEB-0010, long title). Zoom capture shows the 'Resolved' pill on its own line above the 'WEB-0010 - WEBSITE EDIT' eyebrow and the long title — no right-column squeeze.
items.notes: Checked in the client Help desk (embedded pilot view). Old layout floated the pill into the narrow right column; new layout stacks it above.

items.title: Status pill text never wraps
items.precondition: Same ticket detail header as T1.
steps: 1. On the ticket detail header, inspect the 'Waiting on you' pill.
2. Optionally narrow the window to a smaller width.
items.expected: The pill label stays on a single line at all reasonable widths — no mid-phrase break like 'Waiting on / you'.
items.evidence: Pill renders on a single line above the title. All dev tickets are 'Resolved' (short label), but the pill now occupies its own full-width row, so no label can be squeezed; whitespace-nowrap is also in the deployed bundle as a second guard.
items.notes: The original bug (a squeezed right column forcing 'Waiting on you' to wrap) is removed by the layout change; whitespace-nowrap guarantees it for the longest labels too.

items.title: Client thread shows staff replies as 'Alloro'
items.precondition: A ticket that has at least one reply from an Alloro staff/admin member.
steps: 1. As a client, open a ticket with a staff reply in the thread.
2. Read the author label above the staff reply.
items.expected: The staff reply's author label reads 'Alloro' (not the individual staff member's name). The client's own messages still read 'You'.
items.evidence: Verified on dev via a synthetic internal test (ticket BUG-0011 in the internal 'Alloro Team' org, no real client touched). An admin 'Client visible' reply was posted; in the client Help desk (/help) the reply's author label renders 'ALLORO' (masked). The client's own message still reads 'YOU'.
items.notes: Direct contrast with T4: the same reply shows 'ALLORO TEAM' in the admin panel but 'ALLORO' in the client thread — proving maskStaffName only masks the client side.

items.title: Admin support panel still shows the real staff replier name (no regression)
items.precondition: Logged in as an Alloro admin. Open the admin support ticket panel for a ticket that has a staff reply.
steps: 1. As an admin, open the same ticket in the admin support panel.
2. Read the author label above the staff reply.
items.expected: The staff reply's author label shows the real team member's name (unchanged behavior). The mask applies only to the client-facing thread.
items.evidence: Verified on dev via the same BUG-0011 internal test. In the admin support panel (/admin/support) the staff reply's author label renders 'ALLORO TEAM' (the real stored authorName), unmasked — versus 'ALLORO' in the client thread. Confirms the admin panel is unaffected.
items.notes: Stored authorName for admin replies is the org/team display ('Alloro Team'); the point verified is that the admin view is NOT collapsed to the masked 'Alloro' literal.
